step_intents: Fetch the Sentry issue to confirm the DatabaseError text and extract stack context.
Scan migrations directory for a migration that creates the referenced column or relation.
Cross-check the table name in migrations to bracket the migration range to apply.
Post the apply-runbook as a comment so a human can execute the psql step; supervisor never writes to prod DB.
Flag the issue so an on-call human picks up the psql apply step.

slots.body: Supervisor runbook: apply migrations that introduce `$slots.table_name.$slots.column_or_relation` on prod. Migration authored in repo (see $s2.matches, $s3.matches) but not applied in prod database. Human action required: run `psql` against prod replica of the affected worker DB and apply migrations N..M in order. Sentry: $slots.sentry_issue_id. If no matching migration is found in $s2/$s3, reclassify this issue to tier:red (migration authoring needed).

step_intents: Confirm the PR only touches package.json (and lockfile) and that the bumped package matches the slot.

Leave a human-readable rationale on the PR before merging.
Squash-merge the dependency bump now that all preconditions hold.

slots.body: Green-tier supervisor approving $slots.bump_kind bump of $slots.package_name from $slots.from_version to $slots.to_version. CI is green and no security advisory blocks.

description: Billing portal endpoint returns HTTP 400, typically because stripe_customer_id is null for a paid user whose Stripe webhook was missed or raced.
step_intents: Fetch the Sentry issue to confirm the 400 error context, affected user tier, and the null stripe_customer_id stack frame.
Read the billing handler to understand the stripe_customer_id lookup path and verify whether a fallback chain exists.
Post a structured triage comment with the root cause, fix pattern, and reference to the handler file.
Mark issue as triaged and flag it for human attention before any code changes land.
Open a draft PR implementing the fallback chain. Red-tier — requires explicit CODEOWNER approval before merge.

slots.body:
**Supervisor triage — billing portal 400 in `$slots.affected_app`**

Sentry: $slots.sentry_issue_id
Error: $s1.exception_type — $s1.exception_value
Affected user context: $s1.user

**Root cause (pattern):** `handlePortal` short-circuits with HTTP 400 when `users.stripe_customer_id` is `null`. This happens when:
- The Stripe `customer.created` webhook was missed or raced with session creation
- The user was created before the webhook backfill logic was deployed (legacy accounts)

**Fix pattern (from HumanDesign PR #152):**
Replace the hard `if (!user.stripe_customer_id) return 400` guard with a three-step fallback:
1. `users.stripe_customer_id` (primary)
2. `subscriptions.stripe_customer_id` (webhook-written)
3. `ensureCustomer()` — create/retrieve from Stripe API and backfill the `users` row

**Handler state:** see $s2 for current implementation.

A draft PR implementing the fallback chain will be opened for human review. This is a Red-tier action — Stripe billing code requires CODEOWNER approval before merge.

_Run ID: $RUN_ID_

slots.head: supervisor/billing-portal-400-$slots.affected_app
slots.title: fix(billing): resolve portal 400 when stripe_customer_id is null
slots.body:
Closes #$triggers.issue_number

**Root cause:** `handlePortal` returned HTTP 400 for paid users whose `stripe_customer_id` was `null` in the `users` table — typically caused by a missed or raced Stripe webhook.

**Fix:** Implements a three-step fallback chain:
1. `users.stripe_customer_id`
2. `subscriptions.stripe_customer_id` (webhook-written)
3. `ensureCustomer()` — create/retrieve from Stripe and backfill

Pattern source: HumanDesign PR #152.
Sentry: $slots.sentry_issue_id
Diagnosis: see issue comment $s3.comment_url

**Red-tier notice:** Touches Stripe billing code. CODEOWNER review required before merge.

description: CI fails during npm ci with E401 Unauthorized for @latimer-woods-tech/* private GitHub Packages because NODE_AUTH_TOKEN or packages:read permission is missing from a workflow.
step_intents: Read the failing workflow file to check for NODE_AUTH_TOKEN, packages:read permission, and the token fallback expression.
Post a diagnosis comment showing the exact fix pattern required.
Open a draft PR with the package auth fix applied to the failing workflow file.

slots.body:
**Supervisor diagnosis — CI package auth failure in `$slots.affected_repo`**

Failing workflow: `$slots.failing_workflow_path`
Failure mode: `$slots.failure_mode`

**Root cause (pattern from HumanDesign PRs #133–#136):**
`npm ci` on `@latimer-woods-tech/*` private packages fails with E401 when:
1. `NODE_AUTH_TOKEN` is not set on the install step, OR
2. The workflow `permissions` block is missing `packages: read`, OR
3. The token fallback uses `secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN` instead of `github.token` (wrong context)

**Required fix pattern:**
```yaml
permissions:
  packages: read   # ← add this

steps:
  - name: Install
    run: npm ci
    env:
      NODE_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.PACKAGES_READ_TOKEN || github.token }}  # ← github.token not secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN
```

Current workflow state: $s1.excerpt

A draft PR will apply the fix to `$slots.failing_workflow_path`.

_Run ID: $RUN_ID_

slots.head: supervisor/ci-package-auth-$slots.affected_repo
slots.title: fix(ci): add packages:read and NODE_AUTH_TOKEN to $slots.failing_workflow_path
slots.body:
Closes #$triggers.issue_number

**Root cause:** `npm ci` fails with E401 for `@latimer-woods-tech/*` packages in `$slots.failing_workflow_path` due to `$slots.failure_mode`.

**Fix:**
- Adds `packages: read` to workflow/job `permissions`
- Sets `NODE_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.PACKAGES_READ_TOKEN || github.token }}` on the install step
- Corrects fallback from `secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN` to `github.token` if applicable

Pattern source: HumanDesign PRs #133–#136.
Diagnosis: $s2.comment_url

description: CSP violation caused by a stale inline-script SHA-256 hash in the Cloudflare Pages _headers file after a FOUC or inline script was changed.
step_intents: Verify the old hash exists in the _headers file and confirm the full CSP header context.

slots.body:

File: `$slots.headers_file`
Old hash (stale): `$slots.old_hash`
New hash (browser-reported): `$slots.new_hash`

**Root cause:** An inline script was updated (e.g. FOUC theme/density script) but the corresponding `sha256-*` hash in `Content-Security-Policy` was not updated.

**Fix:** Replace `$slots.old_hash` with `$slots.new_hash` in `$slots.headers_file`. Single-line change.

Current _headers excerpt:
```
$s1.excerpt
```

Opening a draft PR with the single-line hash swap.

_Run ID: $RUN_ID_

slots.head: supervisor/csp-hash-$slots.affected_app
slots.title: fix(csp): update inline-script sha256 hash in _headers
slots.body:
Closes #$triggers.issue_number

**Root cause:** Stale CSP hash after inline-script change (e.g. FOUC script updated in a recent PR but `_headers` not updated).

**Fix:** Replaces `$slots.old_hash` with `$slots.new_hash` in `$slots.headers_file`. Single-line, no logic changes.

**Risk:** Green — static Cloudflare Pages headers file only.

Diagnosis: $s2.comment_url
Pattern source: HumanDesign PR #74.

description: Mobile viewport layout regression — typically a CSS grid/flex issue at ≤768px or ≤390px causing elements to overflow, stack incorrectly, or become unreachable by touch.
step_intents: Read the CSS file to understand the existing breakpoint rules and locate the grid/flex context causing the regression.
Post a diagnosis comment with the known fix pattern for this breakpoint and layout kind.
Open a draft PR with the minimal CSS fix for the mobile layout regression.

slots.body:
**Supervisor diagnosis — mobile layout regression in `$slots.affected_app`**

Breakpoint: `$slots.breakpoint`
CSS file: `$slots.css_file`
Issue kind: `$slots.layout_issue_kind`

**Known fix patterns (from HumanDesign PRs #150, #125, #119, #56):**
- `grid-overflow`: add `min-width: 0` to the 1fr grid child; add `max-height` cap on media-child wraps at narrow widths
- `nav-links-reachable-offscreen`: add `visibility: hidden` to the closed off-screen drawer; add `overflow-x: hidden` on the page root at `≤$slots.breakpoint`
- `hero-column-blowout`: set `order: 2` (not `order: -1`) so video renders below text; add `width: 100%` on the video wrap
- `tap-target-too-small`: wrap `<input>` in a 44×44px `<span>` overlay (WCAG 2.5.5); see PR #119 pattern
- `horizontal-scroll`: add `overflow-x: hidden` to the nearest scrollable ancestor

CSS file excerpt (current state):
```css
$s1.excerpt
```

A draft PR will be opened with the minimal targeted fix at `$slots.breakpoint`.

_Run ID: $RUN_ID_

slots.head: supervisor/mobile-layout-$slots.affected_app
slots.title: fix(mobile): resolve $slots.layout_issue_kind at $slots.breakpoint
slots.body:
Closes #$triggers.issue_number

**Root cause:** CSS layout regression at `$slots.breakpoint` — `$slots.layout_issue_kind` in `$slots.css_file`.

**Fix:** Targeted CSS-only change at the `@media (max-width: $slots.breakpoint)` breakpoint. No JS or markup changes (unless nav toggle requires it).

Pattern source: HumanDesign PRs #150, #125, #119.
Diagnosis: see issue comment $s2.comment_url

**Test plan:**
- [ ] Resize browser to `$slots.breakpoint` — verify the layout issue is resolved
- [ ] Resize to >768px — confirm desktop layout is unaffected
- [ ] CI green on deploy-frontend workflow

description: Stripe checkout fails with 'No such price' or 'No such product' because a price ID env var is missing, set to a placeholder, or points to a test-mode ID in a live-mode context.
step_intents: Fetch the Sentry issue to confirm the exact price ID that Stripe rejected and which handler method called it.
Check wrangler.jsonc to confirm the secret name is declared (not hardcoded as a var) and identify where it is consumed.
Post a structured runbook comment explaining the price ID gap and the wrangler secret put fix.
Label the issue so on-call can find and apply the secret update.

slots.body:
**Supervisor triage — Stripe price ID failure in `$slots.affected_app`**

Sentry: $slots.sentry_issue_id
Error: $s1.exception_value
Rejected price ID: extracted from $s1.exception_value
Secret name: `$slots.secret_name`
Environment: `$slots.environment`

**Root cause (pattern from factory PR #347, HumanDesign):**
`createCheckoutSession` received an empty, placeholder (`price_xxxxxxxxxxxxx`), or test-mode price ID when the Worker is running in live mode. This is a **config/secrets gap**, not a deleted Stripe price.

**Verification steps (human action required):**
1. Check the Stripe Dashboard → Products in the correct mode (live vs. test) for the price ID from the error.
2. Run: `wrangler secret list --name <worker-name>` and verify `$slots.secret_name` is set.
3. If missing or wrong: `wrangler secret put $slots.secret_name` and paste the correct `price_*` ID from Stripe.
4. Deploy and verify `/health` returns 200, then retry checkout.

wrangler.jsonc state: $s2.excerpt

**Supervisor cannot write Worker secrets** (FRIDGE rule 8). Human action required.

_Run ID: $RUN_ID_

slots.body:
**Supervisor plan — governance hardening for `$slots.target_repo`**

Hardening kind: `$slots.hardening_kind`
Target file: `$slots.target_file`

**Known patterns:**
- `branch-protection`: add required-status-checks + dismiss-stale-reviews (factory PR #280 playbook)
- `label-addition`: add label to `.github/labels.yml` and sync via label-sync workflow
- `readme-update` / `claude-md-update`: propagate canonical standing orders from factory to app repo
- `agent-protocol-propagation`: copy `.github/AGENT_PROTOCOL.md` + issue template from factory canonical (HumanDesign PR #122 pattern)
- `workflow-permission`: tighten `permissions:` block on a specific workflow (principle of least privilege)

Current file state: $s2.excerpt

A draft PR will implement the change. Green-tier — no destructive mutations, docs/config only.

triggers.title_pattern: Migration drift|column .* does not exist|relation .* does not exist|migration.*not applied

step_intents: Scan migration files for the missing column/relation to identify which migration creates it.
List all migration files to establish the apply sequence.
Post migration apply runbook with specific file references.
Label issue as triaged, requiring human to apply the migration.

slots.body:
**Supervisor — migration drift runbook for `$slots.affected_app`**

Missing: `$slots.missing_item`
Migration scan result: $s1.matches

**Root cause:** A migration that creates `$slots.missing_item` was merged to the repo but was not applied to the production Neon database.

**Fix (human action required):**
1. Connect to the production Neon branch for `$slots.affected_app` (see `docs/runbooks/database.md`).
2. Run: `npm run migrate` from the app's `workers/` directory with `DATABASE_URL` set to the production connection string.
3. Verify: query `SELECT column_name FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = '<table>'` to confirm the column exists.
4. Monitor Sentry for 15 min post-apply to confirm the error stops.

The supervisor cannot write to production databases (FRIDGE rule 8 — irreversible action). A CODEOWNER must execute this runbook.

If the migration is not in the repo (migration was never authored), file a new `bug` issue tagged `supervisor:approved-source` for the DB migration gap fix template.

_Run ID: $RUN_ID_

step_intents: Fetch Sentry issue to confirm Stripe error code, affected user, and stack frame.
Post a structured triage comment with app-specific Stripe error runbook.
Mark issue as triaged so it doesn't get picked up again.

slots.body:
**Supervisor triage — Stripe error `$slots.stripe_error_code`**

Sentry: $slots.sentry_issue_id
App: $slots.affected_app
Error: $s1.exception_type — $s1.exception_value

**Runbook:**
- `no_such_price` / `no_such_product` — price/product ID in code doesn't exist in Stripe. Check if the live-mode ID was accidentally replaced with a test-mode ID. Look up `$s1.exception_value` in the Stripe dashboard and update the env var or DB row.
- `no_such_customer` — customer record deleted in Stripe or created in wrong mode. Check `stripe_customer_id` column for the user mentioned in the stack trace.
- `card_declined` / `insufficient_funds` — user-facing; no code change needed. Check if webhook handler marks the subscription as `past_due` correctly.
- `invalid_request_error` — inspect the Stripe API call arguments in the stack frame. Common cause: empty/null fields passed to the API.

**Next step:** A CODEOWNER should investigate the linked Sentry issue and apply the appropriate fix. If a code change is needed, file a new issue tagged `supervisor:approved-source` with the specific file + line.

_Run ID: $RUN_ID_

step_intents: Fetch the Sentry issue detail including stacktrace, breadcrumbs, and culprit frame.
Read the implicated source file at the top stack frame to localize the defect line.
Post a structured diagnosis comment on the originating GitHub issue linking Sentry evidence to the source line.
Mark the issue as triaged so downstream automation does not re-enter this template.
Open a draft PR only when fix_mode=auto_patch and a <5 line patch is derivable from the diagnosis; otherwise the orchestrator skips this step and escalates to Red.

User `$slots.user_id` found:

- Confirm reproducibility (device + viewport)

step_intents: Fetch cross-app Sentry error rates and synthetic monitor status to confirm scope of degradation.
Post structured degradation runbook with curl commands and rollback instructions.
Label as triaged.

slots.body:
**Supervisor — worker health degradation triage**

Affected worker: `$slots.affected_worker` (HTTP $slots.error_code)
Observability summary: $s1.summary

**Immediate checks (human):**
1. `curl -I https://$slots.affected_worker.adrper79.workers.dev/health` — confirm current status
2. Check Cloudflare dashboard for the worker at https://dash.cloudflare.com
3. Check Sentry project for `$slots.affected_worker` for recent error spike
4. If 502/503: check if a recent deployment is the cause via `wrangler rollback`

**Rollback command (if deployment-related):**
```
cd apps/$slots.affected_worker && wrangler rollback
```

**Escalation:** If the worker is still degraded after 15 min, page on-call via Pushover.

The supervisor cannot invoke `wrangler rollback` (FRIDGE rule 8 — irreversible infra action). A CODEOWNER must execute this runbook.

_Run ID: $RUN_ID_

step_intents: Read the worker's current wrangler.jsonc to inspect declared bindings.
Verify whether the referenced binding exists in the Cloudflare account and resolve the correct ID if drifted.
Cross-check the worker's deploy status to confirm the drift is live and not mid-deploy.
Open a PR with the corrected binding ID so a human can review and merge.

slots.title: fix(wrangler): correct $slots.binding_kind binding for $slots.worker_name
slots.body: Automated fix: wrangler.jsonc referenced a stale $slots.binding_kind binding. Updated to the resolved ID from cloudflare.listWorkers (see $s2). Current file state captured in $s1.
